assessment_items.question_content:
**The main relief and the smaller reliefs on *Ashurbanipal hunting lions* speak to . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The main relief and the smaller reliefs on these panels speak to the power of the Assyrian kings.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-near-east/v/ashurbanipal-hunting-lions--assyrian)**

choices.content: the power of the Assyrian kings.
the power of the Assyrian gods.
the many nations that pay tribute to the Assyrian kings.
the divine support that the Assyrian kings receive.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Lions, which were native to Mesopotamia, were symbols of [[☃ dropdown 1]].**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0961ea9673b0fe2c198a29c183d91777.png)
hints:
**Lions, which were native to Mesopotamia, were symbols of the violence of nature.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-near-east/v/ashurbanipal-hunting-lions--assyrian)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following features help us to identify the king?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9fea52f6043e55d24660b5674253fe4b.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The hat and his large size help us to identify the king. In Ancient Near Eastern art, the largest figure is always the most important.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-near-east/v/ashurbanipal-hunting-lions--assyrian)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is not true of *Ashurbanipal hunting lions*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The relief shows the king and his guards hunting a lion in an arena under controlled conditions.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-near-east/v/ashurbanipal-hunting-lions--assyrian)**

choices.content: The animals are represented in a more naturalistic way than the people.
Originally, these reliefs would have been brightly painted.
The artist overlapped figures to show depth of space.
The relief shows the king and his guards hunting a lion in the wild.
